name: rawreader
desc: Memory-efficient reader for .cwa, .gt3x and .bin raw accelerometer formats with lazy chunked loading.
problem: Vendor formats are large and inconsistent, blocking reproducible pipelines.
whenToUse: At ingestion, when working with multi-day raw triaxial files from mixed devices.
outputs: Calibrated signal arrays + structured metadata (sample rate, timezone).

name: wearQC
desc: Automated quality-control flags for non-wear, calibration drift and clipping in free-living data.
problem: Unflagged poor-quality segments bias downstream metrics.
whenToUse: Immediately after ingestion, before deriving any activity metrics.
outputs: Per-window quality flags + a participant-level QC report.

name: DeviceSync
desc: Aligns multi-sensor wearable streams (accelerometer, PPG, GPS) to a shared clock.
problem: Sensors drift and start at different times, breaking fusion.
whenToUse: When combining two or more devices per participant.
outputs: Time-aligned long-format dataset with offset diagnostics.

name: nonwear-bench
desc: Benchmark harness comparing non-wear detection algorithms on labelled datasets.
problem: Choosing a non-wear rule is opaque and dataset-dependent.
whenToUse: During protocol design, to pick a non-wear method.

name: ENMOkit
desc: Computes ENMO, MAD and intensity-gradient metrics from raw acceleration at configurable epochs.
problem: Reimplementing standard metrics leads to subtle, study-breaking differences.
whenToUse: After QC, to derive comparable movement metrics.
outputs: Epoch-level ENMO/MAD time series + daily summaries.

name: spectraMove
desc: Frequency-domain features — dominant frequency, spectral entropy — for gait and cadence.
problem: Time-domain metrics miss rhythmic structure in movement.
whenToUse: When cadence or gait regularity is of interest.

name: BoutFinder
desc: Detects sustained activity bouts and sedentary breaks with configurable tolerance.
problem: Bout definitions vary widely and are rarely reproducible.
whenToUse: For accumulation and fragmentation analyses.
outputs: Bout tables with duration, intensity and break counts.

name: epochr
desc: Flexible re-epoching and cut-point application across multiple metric definitions.
problem: Comparing cut-points means rerunning whole pipelines.
whenToUse: During sensitivity analysis of intensity thresholds.

name: PhenoCluster
desc: Unsupervised clustering of 24-hour movement profiles into behavioural phenotypes.
problem: Mean activity hides distinct behavioural patterns.
whenToUse: For exploratory subgroup discovery in a cohort.

name: SleepWakeNet
desc: Deep-learning sleep/wake/nap classifier for wrist accelerometry.
problem: Heuristic sleep algorithms miss naps and fragmented sleep.
whenToUse: When sleep timing and quality are outcomes.
outputs: Per-epoch sleep stage probabilities + nightly summaries.

name: PostureID
desc: Sitting / standing / stepping classification from thigh-worn sensors.
problem: Intensity metrics cannot separate sitting from standing still.
whenToUse: For sedentary-behaviour and standing-time studies.
outputs: Posture time series + daily posture budgets.

name: archetype-viz
desc: Visual diagnostics for comparing and validating discovered movement archetypes.
problem: Clusters are hard to interpret and validate.
whenToUse: After clustering, to characterise and label groups.

name: CompoReg
desc: Compositional regression linking activity composition to health outcomes.
problem: Standard regression mishandles the closed 24-hour day.
whenToUse: For associations between time-use and outcomes.

name: isoSub
desc: Isotemporal substitution modelling toolkit for reallocating time between behaviours.
problem: Stakeholders want "what if" time-swap estimates.
whenToUse: To estimate effects of reallocating time.
outputs: Predicted outcome change per reallocation scenario.

name: causalMove
desc: Target-trial emulation helpers for accelerometer cohort studies.
problem: Observational designs invite confounding and immortal-time bias.
whenToUse: When estimating causal effects from cohorts.

name: ActiPlot
desc: Publication-ready actigraphy heatmaps and 24-hour radial plots.
problem: Raw plots rarely meet publication standards.
whenToUse: When preparing figures for papers or reports.

name: CompoTern
desc: Ternary plots for compositional movement data and substitution results.
problem: Compositional results are hard to show in 2-D.
whenToUse: To visualise three-part time-use compositions.

name: dashMove
desc: Interactive Shiny dashboard template for cohort movement summaries.
problem: Stakeholders want to explore, not read static tables.
whenToUse: For sharing cohort summaries with collaborators.

name: GuideCalc
desc: Maps measured behaviour to WHO physical-activity guideline adherence.
problem: Translating metrics into guideline language is inconsistent.
whenToUse: To report guideline adherence from device data.

name: PolicySim
desc: Simulates population health impact of activity-shift scenarios.
problem: Policy teams need scenario estimates, not just associations.
whenToUse: For "what if the population moved more" projections.
